title: Required executable or runtime binary missing
evidence: exec /__e/node20/bin/node: no such file or directory
steps: Confirm the top diagnosis `missing-executable` by reproducing the failure from the same command or CI step if possible.
Use the matched evidence lines as the starting point for triage: exec /__e/node20/bin/node: no such file or directory.

Inspect the most relevant local files first: Dockerfile, .github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/release.yml, scripts/docker-smoke.sh, scripts/release-build.sh, scripts/resolve-extra-pack.sh, scripts/smoke-release.sh.
Identify which command or runtime path the failing step expects to execute.
Confirm the binary exists in the active runner or container image:
Install the missing package in the job image or switch to an image that already contains the required runtime.
If the path is hard-coded, update the workflow or wrapper script to use the actual installed location.
If the failure happens only inside a containerized CI step, make sure the container image includes the same runtime that the host-based workflow expects.
After the immediate fix, harden the workflow with: Standardize runner or container images for each workflow family.
agent_prompt:
You are helping resolve a deterministic CI failure in the local repository.
Workflow schema: workflow.v1.
Top diagnosis: missing-executable - Required executable or runtime binary missing.
Log source: stdin.
Evidence lines:
- exec /__e/node20/bin/node: no such file or directory
Likely files to inspect:
- Dockerfile
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/release.yml
- scripts/docker-smoke.sh
- scripts/release-build.sh
- scripts/resolve-extra-pack.sh
- scripts/smoke-release.sh
Local repro commands:
- command -v <tool>
- ls -l <expected-path>
Recommended fix steps:
- Identify which command or runtime path the failing step expects to execute.
- Confirm the binary exists in the active runner or container image:
- Install the missing package in the job image or switch to an image that already contains the required runtime.
- If the path is hard-coded, update the workflow or wrapper script to use the actual installed location.
- If the failure happens only inside a containerized CI step, make sure the container image includes the same runtime that the host-based workflow expects.
Verification commands:
- command -v <tool>
Work deterministically: inspect the failing path, make the smallest complete change, and verify the exact failure is resolved.
